case_name: Jacob M. Rose, individually and as of the Estate of Jack F. Rose and Jeremy P. Rose, individually v. Oakland Healthcare Management, LLC, d/b/a Oakland Manor

summary.brief: Jacob M. Rose, individually and as of the Estate of Jack F. Rose and Jeremy P. Rose, individually v. Oakland Healthcare Management, LLC, d/b/a Oakland Manor. Medical malpractice case involving neurology.

evidence_index.text: Jack Rose became a resident at Oakland Manor, a Medicaid-participating skilled nursing facility, in February 2019.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Rose was removed from the facility and hospitalized for a medical condition (the record is not clear as to the condition).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Rose tested negative for COVID-19 on July 7 and 10.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Rose was hospitalized for a suspected stroke on July 23. He tested positive for COVID-19 on the day he was admitted to the hospital.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The death certificate listed Rose’s immediate cause of death as COVID-19 infection due to acute hypoxic respiratory failure and bacterial pneumonia.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The CMS Report identified deficiencies such as improper sanitization of the sit-to-stand lift, the failure to complete hand hygiene, the failure to wear complete personal protective equipment (PPE), and the failure to completely seal an isolation area.
evidence_index.speaker: CMS Report

evidence_index.text: Mincer’s expected opinion testimony, as set forth in the disclosure, was based on reading the death certificate and the CMS Report.
evidence_index.speaker: Jaylin Mincer

evidence_index.text: The district court dismissed the suit on the defendant’s motion for summary judgment, holding the plaintiffs failed to establish reckless or willful misconduct.
evidence_index.speaker: District court

evidence_index.text: The plaintiffs failed to support their resistance to the motion for summary judgment with evidence sufficient to create a triable issue of fact for a jury.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Jack Rose was hospitalized for a suspected stroke on July 23, 2020, and tested positive for COVID-19 on the day of admission.
initial_state.initial_working_problem: Suspected stroke with concurrent COVID-19 infection

reveal.decision_impact: Requires isolation and specific management for COVID-19

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with suspected stroke and tests positive for COVID-19

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in nursing facility during COVID-19 pandemic
decision_points.prompt: What infection control measures should be implemented?

options.description: Strict adherence to CDC and CMS COVID-19 guidelines
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard infection control measures to prevent COVID-19 spread
Failure to consistently follow guidelines increases risk of infection

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant failed to consistently follow infection control guidelines
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Deficiencies in sanitization and PPE use

expected_action_court.description: Strict adherence to infection control guidelines should have been followed

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to adhere to infection control guidelines increased risk of COVID-19 spread
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Strict adherence to guidelines to prevent infection
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by CMS and CDC guidelines

patient_outcome.description: Patient died from COVID-19 infection due to acute hypoxic respiratory failure and bacterial pneumonia

key_findings: Plaintiffs failed to establish recklessness or willful misconduct
Deficiencies identified did not rise to the level of recklessness

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to establish recklessness or willful misconduct

factual_timeline.event: Patient hospitalized for suspected stroke and tested positive for COVID-19

complications.complication: Acute hypoxic respiratory failure and bacterial pneumonia

allegations.allegation: Failure to follow COVID-19 infection control guidelines

court_findings.finding: Defendant did not act recklessly or with willful misconduct
